Matter is anything that has mass and occupies some space. Examples of matter include iron, water, air, plastic, wood, stone, oil, steam, etc. Matter can be found in three main forms, i.e., solid, liquid and gas Matter is composed of elements. subtopic: 3. Interconversion Of Potential Energy And Kinetic Energy
content:
You have observed that potential energy can change into kinetic energy and vice versa. In the absence of friction, the sum of kinetic and potential energies (mechanical energy) remains conserved. It means that decrease in potential energy is exactly equal to increase in kinetic energy and vice versa but total mechanical energy must remain unchanged. This is known as the conservation law of mechanical energy Let us consider the following examples for interconversion of potenti
